speaker: Narrator
text: There was an increase of around 2 degrees for the water outside, 10 degrees for you stupid yanks.

speaker: Narrator
text: This will add an unfortunate number of complications for our friends: the fish. These creatures, as close to us as they are, our sustainers, our friends, are assaulted on all sides by parasites.

speaker: Narrator
text: Parasites; You might only consider them as the vampiric creatures that suck blood, such as ticks or mosquitoes. In ecological science, we consider anything that harms others to be a parasite. Even we, the human race, are ultimately parasites.

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina, a young woman whose love of fish and fish parasites has been barely enough to get her through several years of grinding university work. She is joined by an acquaintance and classmate, Blanche, who seemed to be the only one in her class who wasn't scared off by her intimadating aura.

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina always wore earbuds when she was out. To ensure that no one, absolutely no one, had an inkling of an idea that she should be approached. Even now, Blanche can tell that she's been up for hours already at 8 in the morning, and she can smell that if she had showered in the intervening hours, she hadn't done it well enough. Layer after layer of defense.

speaker: Blanche
text: I'll take the rods and the net.

speaker: Blanche
text: Could you handle the... fishcooler and the bait?

speaker: Christina
text: Livewell, fish cooler, whatever...

speaker: Blanche
text: I'll take it, yeah.

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina sucks her teeth and puts her phone back into her pocket. She's been watching her favorite v-tuber on the way to the park. The one benefit of this project was having an excuse to get up at ungodly hours of the morning when she could just about catch the end of their livestreams.

speaker: Narrator
text: Tyresta National Park wasn’t the ideal place to do our research. They're both science undergrads enrolled at Stockholm University, where they are paired together for a project in a summer course. Unlike Christina, Blanche had truly blossomed during her college years.

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina was always flabbergasted that someone like her needed to rush into the disappointments of adulthood. That you'd wish to be free of the university playground and instead fend for themselves as a full-blown working taxpayer...

speaker: Blanche
text: Christina!

speaker: Blanche
text: You seem tired.

speaker: Christina
text: What?!

speaker: Blanche
text: Is that too much for you to carry?!

speaker: Christina
text: I'm just taking my time, it's nice out!

speaker: Narrator
text: They reach the spot and set up the fish cooler and the maggot-filled bait box, taking across the river opening to the sea, and wait for the little fish to bite.

speaker: Blanche
text: Once again, you don’t need to handle the fish like that. I hope I'm not insisting too much.

speaker: Christina
text: What kind of research can be done if we don't periodically inspect the fish?

speaker: Christina
text: It's a bad enough idea to put the sick and the healthy together in the same water when-

speaker: Blanche
text: Oh, a bite!

speaker: Narrator
text: They needed to study something that would be exposed to pathogens in both salt and fresh water, wasn’t particularly valuable to industry, and, above all else, Christina found to be a little cute. Mort, the common roach. A  little fish that hides among the underwater plants in fresh, brackish, or ocean waters, unable to decide whether it prefers the sea or the river. It is tasteless to everyone but the Finns.

speaker: Blanche
text: You of all people should know, we have plenty of examples already living at the aquarium to measure against.

speaker: Christina
text: A pampered creature with no notion of the struggle of everyday life cannot be used as a real control in a proper study.

speaker: Blanche
text: Oh, oh, I suppose so!

speaker: Christina
text: There's the fucker!

speaker: Narrator
text: First fish, healthy on the outside, quite clean as if it knew of hygiene, as much as a fish could. Blanche has stepped into the water herself. The two of them catch fish after fish. About half of them are as perfect a specimen of roach as could be. Shiny, healthy, happy. The other half...

speaker: Blanche
text: These... uh creeps?

speaker: Christina
text: Creeps, are you afraid of fish now?

speaker: Blanche
text: Some of these fish have been infested with a parasite in the eye, haven't you noticed?

speaker: Christina
text: What looks like rapid eye movements in the pupil seems to be the work of another creature that's living within the fish's eyes.

speaker: Christina
text: Something I wrote down about 15 minutes ago, what took you so long?

speaker: Blanche
text: [wave]Let's catch more fish~.[wave]

speaker: Narrator
text: Another round of fish caught. Some of the catch in the livewell have regained their composure, darting around in the water.

speaker: Christina
text: I'm thinking...

speaker: Blanche
text: Oh, Miss Christina Nobel has an idea, please enlighten me!

speaker: Christina
text: Hold on, I can't hear you over my Pasa- I mean my podcast.

speaker: Blanche
text: [i]Oh, a podcast?[/i]

speaker: Narrator
text: Blanche scoffs as she murmurs to herself in annoyance.

speaker: Christina
text: We're throwing back the healthy ones and leaving the sick. We'll never catch enough to do a proper study anyway.

speaker: Blanche
text: So we'll do what I wanted us to do from the very start?

speaker: Christina
text: Sure, sure.

speaker: Christina
text: I'll take care of separating the ones we have now.

speaker: Blanche
text: With your bare hands?

speaker: Christina
text: Shouldn’t be an issue.

speaker: Christina
text: Even the predators here don’t take much interest in mammals small enough for them to eat.

speaker: Narrator
text: Chrisina bravely separates the fish. Carefully observing the eye and other areas for signs of disease and freeing them. Then, something quite odd happens. I feel healthy. With boldness, she drives her hand into the fish-filled cooler and clamps down on the roach. Until, with her hand below the water, she felt something. Not just the warmth of the water, not just the fish. Christina screams.

speaker: Christina
text: AGH!

speaker: Narrator
text: Blanche drops the roach between her fingers.

speaker: Blanche
text: What happened?

speaker: Blanche
text: There's no telling what kind of disease is in the water, are you okay?

speaker: Christina
text: I think so.

speaker: Narrator
text: She flashes her hand at Blanche, which seems untouched. The urge to jokingly flip off Blanche crossed her mind but she ultimately decided not to. Everything looks fine.

speaker: Christina
text: They're all healthy, baby smooth too.

speaker: Blanche
text: We'll see.

speaker: Blanche
text: Sigh, we don't need that many more fish; we’ll just wrap up earlier than we expected today.

speaker: Christina
text: Good, I  have other things to attend to.

speaker: Blanche
text: A job?

speaker: Christina
text: It's more important than that.

speaker: Christina
text: Mmmf.

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina winces from pain. On the tip of her finger, she can feel something, writhing inside that small pinhole. The slight saltiness of the water surrounding the wound stung. She thinks to herself that she can cope with the pain if she had some alone time to herself. Pasaya could make her feel better, she's probably live right now.

speaker: Narrator
text: They finished gathering the Mort. About 80% were relatively normal, and 20%  were creeps. Christina can still feel it. Something is crawling up through her veins, brushing up against the walls of her bloodstream clumsily. As if a child were running their hand down the wooden walls of an old house. It’s getting harder to breathe.

speaker: Christina
text: Hahh, hahh.

speaker: Christina
text: Good... work...

speaker: Narrator
text: Moving the livewell was harder now that it was filled with fish water. Christina can feel something wrong as she takes one omninous step towards the car park. Blanche sees her sweating. It was hard to tell what was drenched by the river and what was drowned out by perspiration.

speaker: Blanche
text: You were lively earlier today, but now I can see the maggots getting to you in a week.

speaker: Christina
text: I'm fine.
